_meta.description: North America subset of the retirement city dataset. All scores are 1-10 (10 = best). Monthly costs in USD for a single person, comfortable lifestyle.

schema_notes.scores: All *_score fields are 1-10 integers. 10 = best/most favorable for retirees.
schema_notes.monthly_cost: USD, single person, comfortable lifestyle (not budget, not luxury).
schema_notes.visa_ease_score: 10 = easy long-stay visa or retirement visa available. 1 = very restrictive. US and Canadian cities score 10 for citizens/permanent residents; 5-6 for foreign retirees (standard tourist/visitor rules apply with no dedicated retirement visa in the US).
schema_notes.healthcare_score: 10 = world-class private hospitals, low cost. 1 = poor infrastructure.
schema_notes.safety_score: 10 = very safe. 1 = high crime or instability.
schema_notes.climate_score: 10 = year-round pleasant. Subjective; filter by climate_type.
schema_notes.overall_retirement_score: Weighted composite. Recalculate when user adjusts priority weights.
schema_notes.ids: Integer ids here are local to this North America file (1-50). The master table uses UUID primary keys, so renumber or drop on import as needed.

_meta.supabase_table_sql: CREATE TABLE retirement_cities (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), city TEXT NOT NULL, country TEXT NOT NULL, region TEXT NOT NULL, subregion TEXT, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, monthly_cost_usd INTEGER, lifestyle_label TEXT, description TEXT, tags TEXT[], visa_ease_score INTEGER, healthcare_score INTEGER, safety_score INTEGER, climate_score INTEGER, expat_community_score INTEGER, internet_score INTEGER, overall_retirement_score FLOAT, climate_type TEXT, language TEXT, currency TEXT, time_zone TEXT, popular_neighborhoods TEXT[], visa_notes TEXT, healthcare_notes TEXT, best_for TEXT[], avoid_if TEXT[], expense_shares JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW());

cities.description: Sun-drenched desert city surrounded by five mountain ranges — affordable, artsy, and with a thriving retiree community drawn by 350+ sunny days a year.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens or permanent residents. Foreign retirees enter on B-2 tourist visa (up to 6 months); no dedicated US retirement visa exists.
cities.healthcare_notes: Banner University Medical Center and Tucson Medical Center offer strong care. University of Arizona medical school drives research quality.
avoid_if: Those sensitive to extreme summer heat (110°F+)
Anyone needing a major-metro specialist network

cities.description: America's fifth-largest city — a sprawling Sunbelt hub with world-class golf, excellent medical infrastructure, and a massive retiree population.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens or permanent residents. Foreign retirees enter on B-2 tourist visa (up to 6 months).
cities.healthcare_notes: Mayo Clinic Phoenix, Banner Health system, and Dignity Health provide world-class care. One of the strongest medical networks in the Sunbelt.
best_for: Those wanting a large retiree social scene
avoid_if: Those who dislike sprawl or car dependence
Anyone sensitive to extreme summer heat

cities.description: Arizona's upscale desert resort city — acclaimed spas, galleries, and golf courses in a polished Sonoran Desert setting.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. Foreign retirees use B-2 tourist visa; no US retirement visa program.
cities.healthcare_notes: HonorHealth and Mayo Clinic nearby. Excellent specialist access and concierge medical practices.
avoid_if: Budget-focused retirees
Those who dislike intense summer heat

cities.description: Spiritual red-rock sanctuary in northern Arizona — extraordinary scenery, New Age wellness culture, and world-class hiking just two hours from Phoenix.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. Foreign retirees enter on B-2 visa. Small-town access means no on-site immigration services.
cities.healthcare_notes: Verde Valley Medical Center handles routine care; serious conditions require travel to Flagstaff or Phoenix hospitals.
best_for: Those wanting small-town safety and beauty
avoid_if: Those needing specialized or emergency healthcare locally
Budget retirees (tourist prices inflate local costs)

cities.description: High-altitude mountain town at 7,000 ft — cool summers, skiing, dark skies, and Northern Arizona University energy in a Ponderosa pine forest.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Flagstaff Medical Center (part of Northern Arizona Healthcare) provides solid regional care including cardiac and cancer services.
avoid_if: Those who struggle with altitude or cold winters
Retirees wanting warm year-round weather

cities.description: America's oldest capital city — adobe architecture, world-class art galleries, opera, and a distinctive Pueblo-influenced culture at 7,000 ft.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Christus St. Vincent Regional Medical Center offers comprehensive care. Some specialists require trips to Albuquerque.
best_for: Those wanting four seasons without extreme heat
avoid_if: Those on tight budgets (high cost for New Mexico)
Anyone needing a full specialist network locally

cities.description: New Mexico's largest city on the Rio Grande — affordable high desert living, 310 sunny days, hot air balloons, and a growing arts scene.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: UNM Health Sciences Center and Presbyterian Healthcare provide university-level and comprehensive regional care.
best_for: Those wanting sunshine and outdoor access
avoid_if: Those concerned about property crime (above US average)
Anyone wanting a walkable urban environment

cities.description: Entertainment capital of the world — no state income tax, world-class dining and shows, affordable housing, and surprising proximity to national parks.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees. Nevada has no state income tax — a major financial draw.
cities.healthcare_notes: University Medical Center and several large hospital systems. Growing medical infrastructure; some specialist gaps remain.
best_for: Those wanting affordable housing with big-city amenities
avoid_if: Those sensitive to extreme summer heat
Anyone wanting a quieter, small-town atmosphere

cities.description: Las Vegas's safe, master-planned neighbor — consistently ranked one of America's safest cities with excellent parks, trails, and no state income tax.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Nevada's no income tax policy applies.
cities.healthcare_notes: St. Rose Dominican and Dignity Health hospitals provide strong local care; Las Vegas medical complex is minutes away.
best_for: Those wanting suburban comfort with Las Vegas access
avoid_if: Those wanting an urban walkable environment
Anyone who dislikes suburban sprawl

cities.description: Utah's fastest-growing city — red rock beauty, mild winters, gateway to Zion and Bryce Canyon, and one of the sunniest spots in the US.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Intermountain St. George Regional Medical Center is a full-service hospital; growing specialist base to meet rapid population growth.
avoid_if: Those wanting big-city nightlife or culture
Retirees needing advanced specialist care locally

cities.description: Idaho's vibrant, fast-growing capital — outdoor recreation on the Boise River Greenbelt, a thriving food scene, and four true seasons without extreme heat.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Saint Alphonsus Regional Medical Center and St. Luke's Health System provide excellent regional care including heart and cancer centers.
best_for: Those wanting four seasons without extreme heat
Retirees seeking an affordable, growing city
avoid_if: Those wanting coastal living
Anyone sensitive to wildfire smoke in late summer

cities.description: Idaho's sparkling lake city — stunning mountain and lake scenery, world-class golf, skiing nearby, and a tight-knit community with no state income tax.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees. Idaho has no state tax on Social Security income.
cities.healthcare_notes: Kootenai Health is a full-service regional medical center; Spokane (WA) is 30 minutes away for advanced specialty care.
avoid_if: Those who dislike cold, snowy winters
Retirees needing immediate access to a major medical center

cities.description: The Blue Ridge Mountains' cultural gem — vibrant arts, acclaimed craft beer scene, mild mountain climate, and a creative retiree community in western North Carolina.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Mission Health (HCA) provides full-service regional care; growing specialist base driven by retiree influx.
best_for: Those wanting mountain beauty without harsh winters
avoid_if: Budget retirees (rapidly rising costs)
Those wanting flat terrain or a warm coast

cities.description: Georgia's storied port city — moss-draped squares, antebellum architecture, coastal access, and a warm, slow-paced Southern lifestyle.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Memorial Health University Medical Center and St. Joseph's/Candler Health System provide strong regional care.
best_for: Those wanting coastal access without Florida prices
avoid_if: Those sensitive to humidity and heat
Anyone wanting a walkable, car-free lifestyle

cities.description: South Carolina's jewel — pastel antebellum homes, world-class restaurants, stunning beaches, and a refined coastal lifestyle steeped in history.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 tourist visa for foreign retirees. South Carolina exempts Social Security and significant retirement income from state taxes.
cities.healthcare_notes: MUSC Health (top academic medical center) and Roper St. Francis provide outstanding care; one of the best healthcare offerings in the Southeast.
best_for: Those wanting a refined coastal lifestyle
avoid_if: Budget retirees (one of the pricier Southern cities)
Those sensitive to humidity and hurricane risk

cities.description: South Carolina's premier resort island — 12 miles of beaches, 23 golf courses, bike-friendly paths, and a genteel retiree community in a lush natural setting.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Gated resort communities are popular with retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Hilton Head Hospital provides local acute care; Savannah and Charleston are 45-60 minutes for advanced specialty care.
avoid_if: Those on tight budgets
Anyone needing immediate specialty medical access

cities.description: Southwest Florida's most affluent coastal city — powder-white beaches, luxury shopping on Fifth Avenue, world-class golf, and no state income tax.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: NCH Healthcare System and Physicians Regional offer strong local care; Cleveland Clinic is within reach via SW Florida.
avoid_if: Budget retirees (one of Florida's most expensive cities)
Those sensitive to heat and humidity

cities.description: Florida's cultural coast — Siesta Key's crystal-quartz beaches, the Ringling art museum, a thriving theater scene, and a well-heeled but accessible retiree community.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida's no state income tax applies.
cities.healthcare_notes: Sarasota Memorial Hospital is a top-ranked regional medical center; Sarasota has one of the highest concentrations of physicians per capita in the US.
avoid_if: Those who dislike summer heat and humidity
Budget retirees

cities.description: Southwest Florida's gateway city — more affordable than Naples, with a revitalized waterfront, access to Sanibel Island, and a large snowbird and retiree population.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida's no state income tax applies.
cities.healthcare_notes: Lee Health system (multiple hospitals) provides solid regional care; growing specialist network serving the large retiree population.
avoid_if: Those in hurricane-risk-averse areas (direct SW Florida exposure)
Anyone wanting urban density

cities.description: Tampa Bay's sunny peninsula city — the most walkable city in Florida, a booming arts district, 361 days of sunshine per year, and vibrant waterfront living.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: Bayfront Health and BayCare Health System provide strong care; Tampa General (nationally ranked) is 20 minutes across the bay.
avoid_if: Those who dislike heat and humidity
Anyone wanting lower density

cities.description: Home to Clearwater Beach — consistently voted America's best beach — with a classic Florida retiree atmosphere and easy Tampa Bay access.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: Morton Plant Hospital (BayCare) is a leading regional medical center; strong cardiac and orthopedic programs.
avoid_if: Those who dislike tourist-heavy beach areas
Anyone wanting urban sophistication

cities.description: Florida's horse country — very affordable, with rolling green hills, natural springs, equestrian estates, and proximity to The Villages mega-retirement community.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: AdventHealth Ocala and HCA Florida Ocala Hospital serve the community; strong network driven by large retiree population.
avoid_if: Those wanting coastal living
Retirees seeking urban amenities

cities.description: America's largest and fastest-growing retirement community — a self-contained city of 55+ residents with golf carts, entertainment squares, and endless amenities.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. Florida has no state income tax. Community is exclusively 55+.
cities.healthcare_notes: UF Health The Villages Hospital and multiple medical centers on-site; designed around the healthcare needs of retirees.
best_for: Active retirees wanting a social community
Those wanting all amenities in one place
avoid_if: Those wanting diversity of age or culture
Anyone wanting urban or coastal living

cities.description: Home of the University of Florida — affordable college-town energy, outstanding healthcare via UF Health, natural springs, and an intellectual, arts-forward community.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: UF Health Shands is a top-ranked academic medical center with world-class specialists across all disciplines.
avoid_if: Those wanting beachside living
Anyone bothered by college-town traffic during football season

cities.description: Florida's capital and a college town — affordable, green and leafy, with canopy roads, state government stability, and easy access to Gulf beaches.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: Tallahassee Memorial and Capital Regional Medical Center provide solid regional care; FSU College of Medicine adds academic resources.
best_for: Those wanting a greener, quieter Florida
avoid_if: Those wanting beaches nearby (1.5 hrs to coast)
Retirees wanting a large retiree social scene

cities.description: The Florida Panhandle's historic naval city — emerald Gulf waters, sugar-white sand on Pensacola Beach, affordable living, and a strong military retiree community.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Florida has no state income tax. NAS Pensacola offers commissary access for military retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Baptist Hospital and Ascension Sacred Heart Pensacola provide strong regional care.
avoid_if: Those wanting to avoid hurricane risk
Retirees needing a major specialty medical hub

cities.description: Texas's vibrant capital — live music, exceptional food, Hill Country access, no state income tax, and a booming economy that keeps amenities first-class.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Texas has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: St. David's Medical Center, Ascension Seton, and UT Dell Medical School provide excellent multi-specialty care.
best_for: Those wanting a dynamic city with no state tax
avoid_if: Budget retirees (rapidly escalating costs)
Those sensitive to extreme summer heat

cities.description: Texas's most visited city — the iconic River Walk, deep military heritage, excellent Mexican food, and a large affordable metro with no state income tax.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Texas has no state income tax. Five military bases create a large commissary-eligible retiree population.
cities.healthcare_notes: University Health, Methodist Health System, and CHRISTUS Health provide outstanding care; strong military medical infrastructure via Brooke Army Medical Center.
avoid_if: Those wanting a cooler climate
Retirees seeking a walkable, dense urban core

cities.description: Texas's Coastal Bend city on the Gulf — affordable waterfront living, windsurfing, sport fishing, and Padre Island National Seashore on its doorstep.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Texas has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: Corpus Christi Medical Center and Christus Spohn Health System provide solid regional care.
avoid_if: Those wanting a major arts or dining scene
Anyone concerned about hurricane risk

cities.description: Texas Hill Country's German heritage gem — acclaimed wine country, wildflower meadows, boutique Main Street charm, and a peaceful retirement atmosphere.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Texas has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: Hill Country Memorial Hospital handles routine care; San Antonio medical centers are 70 miles for advanced care.
avoid_if: Those needing nearby specialty healthcare
Retirees wanting urban nightlife

cities.description: America's most unique city — jazz, Creole cuisine, Mardi Gras, extraordinary architecture, and a vibrant arts scene that never sleeps.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Tulane Medical Center and Ochsner Health System are nationally recognized; strong healthcare despite the city's other challenges.
best_for: Those wanting a truly unique American city
avoid_if: Those worried about crime or natural disaster risk
Anyone sensitive to heat and humidity

cities.description: Mississippi's Gulf Coast gem — white sand beaches, casino entertainment, fresh Gulf seafood, and very affordable living on the Mississippi Sound.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Mississippi does not tax retirement income.
cities.healthcare_notes: Merit Health Biloxi and VA Gulf Coast Veterans provide local care; New Orleans medical complex is 90 minutes for advanced care.
best_for: Budget retirees wanting a beach lifestyle
avoid_if: Those worried about hurricane risk
Retirees seeking urban or cultural sophistication

cities.description: Alabama's white-sand Gulf beach destination — affordable coastal living, excellent fishing and boating, and a growing retiree community on the Alabama Gulf Coast.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Alabama does not tax Social Security income.
cities.healthcare_notes: South Baldwin Regional Medical Center provides local care; Pensacola and Mobile are 45-60 minutes for advanced specialty care.
best_for: Those wanting a quieter beach town
avoid_if: Those needing specialty medical care nearby
Retirees wanting cultural amenities

cities.description: South Carolina's lovingly preserved Lowcountry town — antebellum mansions, tidal marshes, Marine Corps culture, and an intimate waterfront community near Hilton Head.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. SC exempts retirement income significantly from state taxes.
cities.healthcare_notes: Beaufort Memorial Hospital provides solid local care; Savannah and Charleston are 45-70 minutes for advanced care.
best_for: Those wanting a quiet Lowcountry lifestyle
avoid_if: Those needing specialized healthcare locally
Retirees wanting big-city amenities

cities.description: One of the South's fastest-rising cities — acclaimed Reedy River Falls Park, a thriving downtown food and arts scene, and easy Blue Ridge Mountain access.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. SC exempts significant retirement income from state taxes.
cities.healthcare_notes: Prisma Health Greenville Memorial is a comprehensive academic medical center; excellent oncology and cardiac programs.
best_for: Those wanting affordability near the mountains
avoid_if: Those wanting coastal access
Budget-only retirees (costs rising fast)

cities.description: Tennessee's outdoor adventure capital — Lookout Mountain, the Tennessee River Gorge, world-class bouldering, and a revitalized downtown with the world's fastest municipal internet.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Tennessee has no state income tax on wages.
cities.healthcare_notes: CHI Memorial and Erlanger Health System provide strong regional care; strong cardiac and trauma programs.
best_for: Those wanting fast internet and affordability
avoid_if: Those wanting a coastal lifestyle
Retirees seeking a large cultural scene

cities.description: Gateway to Great Smoky Mountains National Park — affordable Tennessee living, University of Tennessee energy, and four-season Appalachian scenery.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Tennessee has no state income tax on wages.
cities.healthcare_notes: UT Medical Center is an academic medical center with broad specialist coverage; University of Tennessee College of Medicine drives quality.
avoid_if: Those wanting an urban coastal lifestyle
Retirees seeking big-city night life

cities.description: America's first national park city — geothermal bathhouses, Ouachita Mountain hiking, Lake Hamilton water sports, and some of the most affordable retirement living in the US.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Arkansas exempts military and Social Security retirement income from state taxes.
cities.healthcare_notes: CHI St. Vincent Hot Springs provides local acute care; Little Rock is 55 miles for advanced specialty care.
best_for: Those wanting a quirky, historic small city
avoid_if: Those needing advanced specialty healthcare locally
Retirees wanting a large, dynamic city

cities.description: Northwest Arkansas's vibrant college city — home to the Razorbacks, Crystal Bridges Museum, Walmart corporate energy, world-class mountain biking, and surprisingly low costs.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Washington Regional Medical Center and the rapidly expanding Mercy Health Northwest Arkansas Campus provide strong regional care.
avoid_if: Those sensitive to cold, snowy winters
Retirees wanting a laid-back, slow-paced city

cities.description: Walmart's hometown transformed into a world-class arts and cycling destination — Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art, the Razorback Greenway, and a booming creative economy.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Mercy Hospital Rogers (adjacent city) and rapidly expanding NWA healthcare infrastructure serve the growing region.
avoid_if: Those wanting coastal or warm-winter climates
Retirees needing major city healthcare immediately

cities.description: Eastern Washington's outdoorsy inland hub — affordable four-season living, Spokane River rapids through downtown, ski resorts within an hour, and a strong healthcare network.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Washington has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: Providence Health and MultiCare Deaconess provide strong regional care; Gonzaga University supports healthcare training quality.
best_for: Those wanting no state income tax
avoid_if: Those wanting coastal weather or Pacific Coast access
Retirees sensitive to cold winters

cities.description: Washington's gateway to everywhere — Puget Sound and Cascade Mountain views, a vibrant arts and food scene, easy access to Vancouver BC, and Western Washington University energy.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Washington has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: PeaceHealth St. Joseph Medical Center provides comprehensive regional care; Seattle hospitals are 1.5 hrs south.
best_for: Retirees who enjoy grey skies and outdoor beauty
avoid_if: Those who dislike frequent rain and overcast skies
Retirees seeking warmer weather

cities.description: Washington's Victorian seaport on the Olympic Peninsula — a wonderfully preserved 1890s downtown, arts community, sailing culture, and ferry access to Seattle.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees. Washington has no state income tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: Jefferson Healthcare is a small critical-access hospital; serious conditions require ferrying to Seattle or driving to Port Angeles.
best_for: Those wanting an intimate, creative community
avoid_if: Those needing immediate access to specialty healthcare
Retirees wanting easy freeway or urban access

cities.description: Central Oregon's outdoor Mecca — 300 days of sunshine, Mt. Bachelor skiing, Deschutes River paddling, world-class craft beer, and a vibrant retiree and outdoor community.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: St. Charles Health System is a strong regional medical center; growing specialist base fueled by retiree and tech-worker influx.
avoid_if: Budget retirees (rapidly rising costs)
Those needing dense urban amenities

cities.description: Oregon's progressive university city — TrackTown USA, exceptional cycling infrastructure, Willamette Valley wine country, and a laid-back cultural scene.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: PeaceHealth Sacred Heart is a comprehensive regional medical center; University of Oregon Health Sciences resources support care.
avoid_if: Those who dislike rain and overcast winters
Retirees wanting warmer or sunnier climates

cities.description: Southern Oregon's sunny Rogue Valley hub — more affordable than Bend, with Crater Lake access, world-class Shakespeare in nearby Ashland, and 200+ sunny days.
cities.visa_notes: No visa required for US citizens. B-2 for foreign retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Asante Rogue Regional Medical Center provides solid comprehensive care including cancer and cardiac programs.
avoid_if: Those concerned about wildfire smoke risk in summer/fall
Retirees needing major specialist access

cities.description: British Columbia's storied capital on Vancouver Island — Canada's most temperate climate, English garden charm, whale watching, and a vibrant arts and food scene for retirees.
cities.visa_notes: Canadian citizens and permanent residents settle freely. US/foreign retirees may use the Non-Immigrant Route or apply for permanent residency; no dedicated retirement visa but BC Provincial Nominee Program (entrepreneur/investor stream) is an option.
cities.healthcare_notes: BC's universal Medicare covers all residents; Royal Jubilee Hospital and Victoria General provide strong regional care. Wait times are the key challenge.
best_for: Those wanting the mildest climate in Canada
avoid_if: Those wanting warm sunny summers
Budget retirees (high CAD housing costs)

cities.description: BC's Okanagan wine country gem on Okanagan Lake — Canada's warmest summers, 200+ wineries, skiing at Big White, and a thriving retirement community in a beautiful valley.
cities.visa_notes: Canadian citizens and PRs settle freely. Foreign retirees must pursue immigration pathways; BC PNP or federal Express Entry are options. No dedicated retirement visa.
cities.healthcare_notes: Kelowna General Hospital provides comprehensive care; some advanced specialties require Vancouver (4 hrs).
best_for: Those wanting Canadian living with warm summers
avoid_if: Those concerned about increasing wildfire risk in the Okanagan
Non-Canadian retirees facing immigration hurdles

cities.description: Vancouver Island's second city — more affordable than Victoria, ferry-linked to Vancouver, mild year-round climate, and a growing outdoor and arts retirement community.
cities.visa_notes: Canadian citizens and PRs settle freely. Foreign retirees need immigration pathways. BC PNP or federal Express Entry are main routes.
cities.healthcare_notes: Nanaimo Regional General Hospital provides solid regional care; Victoria is 1.5 hrs south for advanced specialty care.
best_for: Those wanting affordable Vancouver Island living
avoid_if: Those wanting big-city amenities
Non-Canadian retirees facing immigration hurdles

cities.description: The Comox Valley's relaxed retirement haven — one of Vancouver Island's most affordable cities, ski resort Mt. Washington nearby, excellent fishing, and a warm community feel.
cities.visa_notes: Canadian citizens and PRs settle freely. Foreign retirees need immigration pathways; no Canadian retirement visa.
cities.healthcare_notes: The Comox Valley Hospital (opened 2017) is a modern regional facility; Campbell River and Victoria handle advanced specialty cases.
best_for: Those wanting a quiet, safe community
avoid_if: Those wanting urban amenities or cultural diversity
Non-Canadian retirees facing immigration hurdles
